title: Fix new <obj> shallow copy not preserving polymorphic runtime type (#7105)
body:
## Summary
`new <handle>` (shallow copy constructor, IEEE 1800-2017 §8.7) now preserves the runtime type of the source object. Previously, copying through a base-class handle produced a base-class object (C++ object slicing), losing derived-class members and virtual dispatch.

## Issue
Fixes #7105 

## Changes
- `include/verilated_types.h`: Added virtual `vlClone()` to `VlClass` base class; added `VlClassRef::vlClone(VlDeleter&)` that performs polymorphic copy via virtual dispatch and `dynamic_cast`
- `src/V3EmitCHeaders.cpp` (`emitInternalMethodDecls`): Generate `vlClone()` override for each concrete (non-abstract, non-interface) class, returning `new ClassName(*this)`
- `src/V3EmitCFunc.h` (`visit(AstNewCopy*)`): Changed emission from `VL_NEW(Type, *rhsp)` to `(rhsp).vlClone(vlSymsp->__Vm_deleter)` for runtime-polymorphic copying

## Test
- Added `test_regress/t/t_class_new_copy_polymorphism.{v,py}` — tests copy through base handle to Derived, GrandChild, and Base objects; verifies virtual dispatch and independent copy semantics

---
Written by Claude Code and PlanV GmbH.

Reviewed by YilouWang.
